name: xschem-viewer-configurable
description: View XSchem .sch/.sym schematics in VS Code with configurable library search paths. Bundles the SKY130 and IHP SG13G2 device symbols so PDK schematics render with zero config, and can follow an xschemrc into an installed open PDK.

scripts.test: node test/resolver.test.cjs && node test/config.test.cjs && node test/manifest.test.cjs

xschem.libraryPaths.markdownDescription:
Additional XSchem library search roots (behaves like `XSCHEM_LIBRARY_PATH`). Each entry is a **directory**; a symbol reference such as `sg13g2_pr/sg13_lv_nmos.sym` is looked up as `<root>/sg13g2_pr/sg13_lv_nmos.sym`. Roots are searched in order, after the extension's bundled libraries and before the schematic-relative fallback. Entries that don't resolve to an existing directory are skipped (enable `#xschem.resolveDebug#` to log skips).

Variables: `${env:VAR}`; a leading `~`; `${workspaceFolder:NAME}` (a **named** folder from a multi-root workspace — the unambiguous form); and bare `${workspaceFolder}` (the opened schematic's own workspace folder — in a multi-root workspace this is the innermost/most-specific folder, e.g. a submodule root, **not** the meta-root). Absolute paths are used verbatim; relative paths resolve against the schematic's workspace folder.

Example (this workspace):
```json
"xschem.libraryPaths": [
  "${env:PDK_ROOT}/ihp-sg13g2/libs.tech/xschem",
  "${workspaceFolder:platform}/docker/xschem_library"
]
```
Or with absolute paths (never ambiguous):
```json
"xschem.libraryPaths": [
  "/home/you/pdks/ihp-sg13g2/libs.tech/xschem",
  "/abs/path/to/spicexplorer-platform/docker/xschem_library"
]
```
(In-repo `shared/…` and sibling-block symbols are picked up automatically by `#xschem.autoDetectXschemrc#`.)

xschem.autoDetectXschemrc.markdownDescription: Walk up from the opened schematic's folder to the workspace root and add every directory that contains an `xschemrc` file as a library search root, **and** parse that file's `append XSCHEM_LIBRARY_PATH` lines (resolving the `[file dirname [info script]]` idiom). So in-repo `shared/…` and sibling-block symbols resolve with no manual configuration. Parsed roots are only added if they exist and lie inside the workspace; lines using `$env(...)` or `source` are skipped — PDK symbols outside the repo stay opt-in via an explicit `#xschem.libraryPaths#` entry.

xschem.followXschemrcPdkSource.markdownDescription:
**Opt-in.** When an in-repo `xschemrc` `source`s an **open-source PDK's** xschemrc — e.g. `source $env(PDK_ROOT)/$env(PDK)/libs.tech/xschem/xschemrc` — add that PDK's `libs.tech/xschem` directory as a library search root, so PDK devices resolve without a manual `#xschem.libraryPaths#` entry.

Requires `PDK_ROOT` to be set in the environment, and the PDK name to come either from an inline `set ::env(PDK) …` in the `xschemrc` or a `PDK` environment variable. Only **open** PDKs are followed: the resolved `…/<pdk>/libs.tech/xschem` path segment must match `sky130*`, `gf180mcu*`, `ihp-sg13g2`, or `sg13g2` — any other (proprietary/foundry) PDK is refused, as is a path with an unresolved variable.

This deliberately reaches **outside** the workspace, which is why it is off by default; leave it off and list the path in `#xschem.libraryPaths#` if you prefer to be explicit. Independent of `#xschem.autoDetectXschemrc#`, which only ever adds in-workspace directories.

xschem.includeWorkspaceFolders.markdownDescription:
When enabled, allow the viewer to load symbol/schematic files from the schematic's **own** workspace folder (enabling relative `../` sub-block references anywhere within that folder). Off by default to keep the webview's file-read scope minimal — the schematic's directory plus the configured `#xschem.libraryPaths#` (and auto-detected `xschemrc` roots) are always available regardless of this setting.

Only the schematic's own workspace folder is ever exposed, never sibling roots of a multi-root workspace. A schematic opened from **outside** every workspace folder has no owning folder, so this setting adds nothing for it — use `#xschem.libraryPaths#` in that case.

xschem.resolveDebug.markdownDescription:
Log every symbol-resolution attempt, to debug "symbol not found" problems and see exactly which paths were tried.

Output lands in **two different consoles**:

- **Resolution attempts** (`'ref' <- bundled …`, `'ref' <- libraryPath …`, `NOT FOUND …`) are logged by the viewer inside the webview. Open that console with **Developer: Open Webview Developer Tools** from the Command Palette — *Help ▸ Toggle Developer Tools* shows the editor's own console, not the webview's.
- **Skipped/refused paths** (a `#xschem.libraryPaths#` entry that isn't a directory, a PDK `source` refused by the open-PDK allowlist or an unset variable) are logged by the extension host — see *Help ▸ Toggle Developer Tools*, or the **Log (Extension Host)** channel in the Output panel.

See [TROUBLESHOOTING.md](https://github.com/NooriDan/vscode-xschem-viewer-configurable/blob/main/docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md).

author: NooriDan
